blueprint.name: Smart Home Battery
blueprint.description: Generic Home Assistant blueprint for up to four batteries. It allocates discharge from house demand, can trigger opportunistic charging during real export, and writes a signed power target for each battery into a numeric entity, with optional custom actions for integrations that need a separate mode switch.
message.invalid_sensor: House power sensor {{ house_power_sensor }} is unavailable or not numeric.
message.small_delta_skipped: House power sensor delta below trigger threshold:
trace.stop_small_delta: Stop on small house power delta
validation.no_target_entity.suffix: is enabled by its state of charge sensor, but no Target power entity is configured. Add a `number` or `input_number` entity, or clear the state of charge sensor to disable this battery slot.
validation.no_power.suffix: has a target power entity configured, but both maximum power limits are set to 0 W. Set a non-zero Maximum discharge power or Maximum charge power to make this slot usable.
input.house_power_sensor.name: House power sensor
input.house_power_sensor.description: Main house power sensor. This selector only shows power sensors. Use a signed sensor in watts with positive values for import and negative values for export.
input.blocking_entities.name: Blocking entities
input.blocking_entities.description: Optional entities that must all stay off for the blueprint to run. If any selected entity is on, unknown, or unavailable, the blueprint can force managed batteries back to neutral only on that state change, then it ignores house-power-triggered runs until every blocker is back to off. This lets an external off-peak charging automation control the same batteries while the blueprint is blocked.
input.battery.soc_sensor.name: State of charge sensor
input.battery.soc_sensor.description: Leave empty to ignore this battery slot. Otherwise, this selector only shows battery sensors that report a charge percentage, and the chosen sensor enables the slot. An enabled slot must also expose a numeric target entity and at least one non-zero power limit.
input.battery.max_discharge_w.name: Maximum discharge power
input.battery.max_discharge_w.descriptio: Manual discharge limit used by the allocator for this battery.
input.battery.max_charge_w.name: Maximum charge power
input.battery.max_charge_w.description: Manual charge limit used by the allocator for opportunistic charging. Charge-capable batteries can absorb residual real export once discharge targets have been trimmed down to the release threshold. Set to 0 if this battery must never be charged by the blueprint.
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.name: Command cooldown
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.descripti: Minimum delay between two active target updates for this battery, and the main signal used to decide how reactive it is. Lower values make the battery handle residual changes first. Higher values make the battery keep a steadier active target while faster batteries absorb demand changes. Set it to 0 to disable the delay entirely. Writing `0`, flipping the sign, or starting from `0` when the raw house meter confirms matching import or export still happens immediately so the blueprint can stop, reverse, or resume a useful battery without waiting. A significant raw export can also reduce an active discharge target before the cooldown expires.
input.battery.target_power_entity.name: Target power entity
input.battery.target_power_entity.descri: The `number` or `input_number` entity written by the blueprint. The target is signed in watts: positive for discharge, negative for charge, `0` for stop. If this battery should charge, the selected entity must accept negative values.
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.name: Actual power sensor
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.descri: Optional power sensor that reports the battery's real output in watts. The allocator still uses the target entity and the house meter as its primary control loop. After the response grace period, a valid non-stale reading is used only as a guardrail to avoid counting a battery as real supply when it is no longer delivering. A configured actual power sensor that becomes non-numeric or has not updated for more than 300 seconds is treated as stale; that battery is kept out of extra discharge allocation until telemetry recovers, while an already active target can stay in place unless a stop, sign change, or real export requires changing it.
input.battery.discharge_actions.name: Discharge actions
input.battery.discharge_actions.descript: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively discharge. They run on every active discharge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_discharge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions.
input.battery.charge_actions.name: Charge actions
input.battery.charge_actions.description: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively charge from surplus export. They run on every active charge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_charge_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions.
